title: US and Iran fail to reach deal after marathon peace talks in Pakistan
subhead: JD Vance said that the Iranians refused to accept Washington's 'terms.'

title: Hungarians vote in closely watched election with Orban's rule on line
Easter truce between Russia and Ukraine falters as Kyiv records '469 violations'
Viktor Orban faces his toughest battle yet for a fifth term
subhead: At 62, the prime minister faces an unprecedented challenge from 45-year-old Peter Magyar, a former civil servant who was virtually unknown two years ago. Forced to abandon the usual closed-door, invitation-only rallies, he has exposed himself, as in Gyor on March 27, to being heckled by opponents.

title: Lebanese mourn victims of 'Black Wednesday': 'We are not just numbers'
subhead: On April 9 in Qamatiyé, near Beirut, the family of Fatima Imtaz buried the 33-year-old pharmacist who was killed by an Israeli airstrike the previous day. That same day, at least 357 people lost their lives in Lebanon, marking the deadliest day recorded since the war between Israel and Hezbollah began on March 2.

title: Crippa, Demise claim Paris marathon victories
subhead: Ethiopia's Shure Demise won the women's elite race, while Italy's Yemaneberhan Crippa took the top spot in the men's equivalent on Sunday.

title: Chambord, the second-most visited château in France, strives to fund much-needed renovation
subhead: The director of the Loire Valley estate, Pierre Dubreuil, has launched a large-scale crowdfunding campaign aimed at the general public in order to complete the renovation budget.

subhead: To tackle public debt, France remains wealthy with solid underlying economic fundamentals. Yet leaders must confront prevailing 'political conservatism' and the wealthiest's behavior, argues economist Thomas Piketty.

title: 'I stared death in the face!' On board the Trans-Siberian, a 9,000-kilometer journey through an exhausted Russia
subhead: 'On Board the Trans-Siberian' (1/2). In late December 2025 and early 2026, on the train linking Moscow to Vladivostok, soldiers returning from Ukraine cross paths with cautious travelers, careful about what they say. Beyond the Urals, propaganda posters attempt by every possible means to recruit new soldiers.

title: The planning battle to revive Tour Montparnasse, Paris's only skyscraper
subhead: After more than 10 years of negotiations between Paris City Hall and the tower's co-owners, the transformation of the now-empty Tour Montparnasse and its property complex is set to begin in autumn 2026.
byline: Audrey Lagadec,  Emeline Cazi and  Isabelle Regnier
